core_principles.title: Calories in vs calories out
core_principles.summary: Fundamental formula for fat loss; non-negotiable. Nervous system strongly influences the calories-out side.

core_principles.title: Nervous system as master controller
core_principles.summary: Neurons innervate fat tissue and control mobilization and oxidation via local epinephrine—not mainly circulating adrenal adrenaline.

core_principles.title: Mobilize then oxidize
core_principles.summary: Lipolysis: break fatty acids from glycerol (lipase), move to bloodstream, then oxidize in mitochondria as ATP.

core_principles.title: Local epinephrine from fat-innervating neurons
core_principles.summary: Fat burning adrenaline comes from neurons connecting to fat, not primarily from adrenal glands into bloodstream.

behavioral_tools.title: Fidgeting / NEAT
behavioral_tools.protocol: Knee bounce, pacing, standing, staccato subtle movements throughout the day. Rothwell & Stock research; 2015/2017 metabolic tracking confirms same food intake + more movement = fat loss.
behavioral_tools.audience: Good entry for exercise-averse or overweight individuals.

behavioral_tools.title: Deliberate cold + shiver (in/out)
behavioral_tools.protocol: 1–5×/week. Cold until shiver → exit 1–3 min without drying → re-enter. ~3 in/out cycles. ~60°F start if not adapted. Shiver releases succinate → brown fat thermogenesis.
behavioral_tools.safety: Extreme cold can shock heart; physician clearance; not adapted = start uncomfortable not ice cold.

behavioral_tools.title: MICT / Zone 2
behavioral_tools.protocol: 20–60 min steady state, 40–60% VO2max or 55–70% max HR.

behavioral_tools.title: High intensity → Zone 2 sequence
behavioral_tools.protocol: 20–60 min high intensity (weights, sprints, kettlebells) then immediately Zone 2 cardio. 3–4×/week. Fasted enhances fat % burned if medically cleared.

behavioral_tools.title: Fasted exercise timing
behavioral_tools.protocol: <90 min moderate exercise: fasted vs fed similar for fat oxidation. ≥90 min MICT: fasted burns more fat after switchover. High intensity 20–60 min: fasted shifts to fat earlier. NOT for performance goals.

compounds.title: Caffeine
compounds.protocol: 100–400 mg, 30–40 min before exercise. Enhances fat oxidation %; up to ~400 mg (~1.5–2 cups; cafes often higher).

compounds.title: Yerba maté
compounds.protocol: Increases GLP-1 → glucagon pathway → more fat oxidation. Best before exercise; also helps at rest.

compounds.title: Berberine / Metformin
compounds.protocol: Lower blood glucose → lower insulin → more fat oxidation. Prescription/medical supervision for metformin.

nutrition.title: Keep insulin low for fat oxidation
nutrition.summary: High insulin blocks fatty acid → ATP in mitochondria. Low carb/moderate carb can help; doesn't require zero carb.

nutrition.title: Adherence over diet type
nutrition.summary: Gardner Lab (Stanford): low-fat, keto, IF etc. all work if deficit + adherence. Pick what you can stick to.

nutrition.title: Pre-exercise nutrition
nutrition.summary: If eating before training: fat/protein spike insulin less than carbs. Can't train fasted → eat; prioritize low insulin if fat loss goal.

safety.title: Medical clearance
safety.summary: Physician approval before fasted exercise and cold/shiver protocols.

safety.title: Cold heart risk
safety.summary: Very cold water can cause heart attack if not adapted.

safety.title: Performance vs fat loss
safety.summary: Fasted/low-insulin protocols optimize fat loss, not athletic performance.
